notes: The most versatile ESP32 development board, with the richest community resources, top choice for beginners

onboard_peripherals.name: LED
onboard_peripherals.notes: Onboard indicator LED. Most projects don't need to control it, GPIO2 can be freed

notes: All I2C/SPI/UART/I2S can be remapped to any free GPIO via GPIO Matrix
GPIO 34-39 are input-only, no internal pull-up/pull-down resistors
GPIO 0 must be pulled high at startup; pulling low enters download mode
GPIO 2/5/12/15 are strapping pins; their level at startup affects operating mode
GPIO 6-11 are occupied by internal Flash, cannot be used externally
ADC2 shares circuitry with WiFi; when WiFi is enabled, ADC2 readings are unreliable. Prefer ADC1 (GPIO 32-33/36-39)
